title: Raw-Categorical Ordered CatBoost Specialist
summary: The current best solutions still hand-engineer categorical information into frequencies and a few composites.
rationale: The current best solutions still hand-engineer categorical information into frequencies and a few composites. CatBoost’s docs explicitly discourage manual one-hot preprocessing and describe ordered boosting plus ordered categorical statistics, which are often stronger for high-cardinality fields like `Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, and their interactions.
implementation_hint: Train a separate CatBoost binary specialist on raw categorical columns (`Driver`, `Race`, `Year`, `Compound`, `Stint`, plus simple interaction columns such as `Driver_Race`, `Race_Compound`, `Driver_Compound`). Keep numerical physics/rule features, but remove most manual categorical frequency engineering in this branch. Use GroupKFold by `Race_Year` so held-out races are never seen in training.
expected_effect: Can recover signal from sparse driver-track-history patterns and categorical interactions that frequency encoding likely smooths away too aggressively.
risk: Rare categories and train/test support mismatch can still overfit or collapse; this branch should be regularized and treated as a specialist for blending, not a guaranteed standalone winner.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
